scenario_results.evidence: Manifest scope, workflow design boundaries, and Microsoft or UiPath authority sources support bounded automation design guidance.
Official queue and transaction sources support actionable queue controls, retries, and exception-handling recommendations.
Connector and service-account guidance supports automation-platform constraint handling while routing custom implementation elsewhere.
Managed environment, DLP, and governance sources support bounded environment and policy recommendations.
Service principal and governance sources support production-safe ownership and credential pattern guidance.
Microsoft and UiPath lifecycle references support release-readiness, communications, rollback, and support planning.
UiPath alerts and Camunda Operate sources support monitoring, incident triage, and escalation recommendations.
Supportability and supported-environment sources cover unsupported runtime or connector-path escalation.
Custom software engineering and middleware implementation are clearly rejected and routed to adjacent specialists.
Manifest adjacency map and routing rules constrain the lane and reject CRM, ERP, HRIS, analytics, and infrastructure ownership.
Manifest and readiness artifacts require explicit uncertainty, missing-context requests, and conservative escalation.
Shared-meta-agent contracts and fallback rules allow safe startup and release review with deferred delegated normalization.

notes: Results are evidence-based and derived from the manifest, package artifacts, and repository delivery contract for issue #141.
Acceptance thresholds are exceeded with 12 scenarios, 12 passes, and a 1.00 pass rate against a 0.92 threshold.
Low-confidence and adjacent-specialty cases are routed rather than guessed.
